problem_addressed: Language model agents lack specialized interfaces for complex software engineering tasks, unlike human engineers who benefit from IDEs.
core_claim: LM agents require specially-built interfaces to effectively perform software engineering tasks, similar to how humans need IDEs.
key_contributions: SWE-agent system that enables LM agents to autonomously use computers for software engineering tasks
Custom agent-computer interface (ACI) that enhances LM agents' ability to create/edit code, navigate repositories, and execute tests
novelty: The custom agent-computer interface (ACI) with LM-friendly commands and environment feedback, specifically designed for software engineering tasks.
method_summary: SWE-agent facilitates LM agents to autonomously use computers through an ACI that includes specific commands for file manipulation and navigation, with structured feedback from the computer environment.
method_components: LM-friendly commands for file operations and navigation
Agent-computer interface connecting LM agent to computer
key_innovation: The ACI design that bridges LM capabilities with software engineering needs through carefully crafted commands and feedback mechanisms.
assumptions: LM agents can benefit from structured interfaces like humans do
Interface design significantly impacts agent performance
headline_result: SWE-agent with GPT-4 resolved 60% of tasks within 25 turns on SWE-bench (full), while Claude 3 Opus resolved 50% within 20 turns on SWE-bench Lite

results_table.value: Resolved instances median <100 steps vs >140 for unresolved
Resolved instances median <$2 vs >$3.5 for unresolved
'create, edit, python' occurred 156 times in turns 1-3

best_result: SWE-agent with GPT-4 resolved 60% of tasks within 25 turns on SWE-bench (full), while Claude 3 Opus resolved 50% within 20 turns on SWE-bench Lite

title: SWE-agent: Agent-Computer Interfaces Enable Automated Software Engineering
